title: Behind The Laughter

jokes.text: Look at her vacuum.
jokes.setup: Documentary discussing the Simpsons' rise to fame
jokes.punchline: Random non-sequitur commentary about someone's vacuum cleaner
jokes.explanation: Random absurd observation that fits the mockumentary format's tendency to include irrelevant talking heads

jokes.text: They told us what to wear... how to dress, which clothes we should put on.
jokes.setup: Homer describing Hollywood's control over their image
jokes.punchline: Three redundant ways to say the same thing about clothing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer redundancy showing his limited vocabulary and thought process

jokes.text: After the show, Meryl Streep spit on me.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing his disastrous Oscar hosting
jokes.punchline: Respected actress Meryl Streep reacting with juvenile disgust
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of high-class actress with crude behavior, perfect economy

jokes.text: They almost drove me to fortified wine.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing the difficulties of raising children
jokes.punchline: Specifically choosing the cheapest, most pathetic type of alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character beat - not just alcohol, but the saddest kind. Great specificity.

jokes.text: That's how I was raised, and I turned out TV.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining parking kids in front of TV was fine
jokes.punchline: Says 'TV' instead of 'fine' - proving TV rotted his brain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-defeating argument where Homer accidentally proves his own point about TV damage through his malapropism

jokes.text: Sheriff Lowbrow. Home Improvement.
jokes.setup: Listing fake TV shows they watched
jokes.punchline: Lists real show 'Home Improvement' among obviously fake lowbrow titles
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig at real TV show by including it with obviously stupid fake shows

jokes.text: Finally, I said, 'So do it. Either [Bleep] or get off the pot.' And [Bleep] he did.
jokes.setup: Marge using crude expression to motivate Homer
jokes.punchline: Narrator takes the metaphor literally
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with narrator taking figurative expression literally, plus Marge swearing is character subversion

jokes.text: Honey, I'm home. The boss is coming to dinner, and I need a clean shirt. I haven't done the laundry yet. Mamma mia! Now I'll have to do it!
jokes.setup: Homer's attempt at realistic family show
jokes.punchline: Painfully cliched sitcom dialogue with Italian stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy showing Homer's terrible writing instincts with generic sitcom cliches

jokes.text: It was amazing how fast Dad betrayed his vision of a realistic show.
jokes.setup: Homer claimed he wanted to do realistic family show
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out his immediate abandonment of realism
jokes.explanation: Lisa's dry observation fits her character and provides meta-commentary on typical creative sellouts

jokes.text: Every day I thought about firing Marge. You know, just to shake things up.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge had good chemistry on screen
jokes.punchline: Homer considered firing his own wife for drama
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's selfishness and obliviousness to family relationships vs. show business

jokes.text: The man who cut my hair was also president of Fox.
jokes.setup: Homer couldn't get past network guards
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Hollywood networking coincidence
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Hollywood's random networking culture and dual careers

jokes.text: Fox had an endless supply of clever slogans, man.
jokes.setup: Discussing Bart's popularity and merchandise
jokes.punchline: Bart sarcastically calls Fox slogans 'clever'
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about The Simpsons' own merchandise, though fairly straightforward sarcasm

jokes.text: Sometimes I'd have to read the cake just to know who I was singing to.
jokes.setup: Bart was invited to every birthday party
jokes.punchline: So many parties he lost track of whose party it was
jokes.explanation: Clever specific detail that captures the overwhelming nature of child stardom

jokes.text: Dad, I've never said 'Cowabunga' in my life. Your script sucks.
jokes.setup: Homer writes dialogue for Bart to say 'Cowabunga'
jokes.punchline: Bart breaks character to criticize the writing
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on catchphrase writing and Bart's actual voice vs. stereotypes

jokes.text: And that horrible act of child abuse became one of our most beloved running gags.
jokes.setup: Homer choking Bart started accidentally during filming
jokes.punchline: Narrator matter-of-factly calls it child abuse but notes its popularity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy addressing the actual disturbing nature of the show's famous running gag

jokes.text: We were using $50 bills as toilet paper and toilet paper as dog toilet paper.
jokes.setup: The family was making lots of money
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous waste escalated to even more ridiculous waste
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing nouveau riche excess taken to absurd extremes

jokes.text: We found a secret room that was filled to the ceiling with parachute pants. I'm wearing some now.
jokes.setup: They moved into MC Hammer's house
jokes.punchline: Homer discovered and is now wearing Hammer's signature pants
jokes.explanation: Great visual image of Homer in parachute pants, good callback to MC Hammer's fall from grace

jokes.text: Yeah, I've seen all the overnight sensations - Brad Hall, Rich Hall, Rich Little, Little Richard - but the Simpsons blew 'em all away.
jokes.setup: Executive discussing Simpson's success vs other acts
jokes.punchline: Chain of names that get progressively less relevant, linked by wordplay
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay chain but fairly predictable structure, shows diminishing returns of entertainment industry

jokes.text: Meanwhile, Krustophenia sits on the shelf.
jokes.setup: Executive discussing hit records
jokes.punchline: Mentions made-up album name that sounds like a disease
jokes.explanation: Funny fake album name that sounds medical/pharmaceutical rather than musical

jokes.text: And the award for Best Hard-core Thrash Metal goes to... Simpsons Christmas Boogie?
jokes.setup: Grammy awards ceremony for metal music
jokes.punchline: Family-friendly Christmas song wins hardcore metal award
jokes.explanation: Strong ironic contrast between musical genres, captures absurdity of award shows

jokes.text: I want to set the record straight. I thought the cop was a prostitute.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing some scandal
jokes.punchline: His defense somehow makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic where his explanation creates more problems than it solves

jokes.text: Oh, Homer was spending money like a... teenage Arab.
jokes.setup: Executive describing Homer's spending habits
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly stereotypical comparison that he seems to know is problematic
jokes.explanation: Problematic stereotype joke that feels cheap, though the pause suggests awareness of inappropriateness

jokes.text: I felt kinda guilty 'cause I was always tryin' to score with his wife.
jokes.setup: Lenny explaining why Homer bought him expensive gifts
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of trying to seduce Marge
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Lenny's casual attitude toward betraying friendship

jokes.text: He paid me and Carl a thousand bucks to kiss each other
jokes.setup: Bart's reckless spending
jokes.punchline: Paying adult men to kiss for his amusement
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor about power dynamics and exploitation

jokes.text: He paid me and Carl a thousand bucks to kiss each other. Hey, did we ever get that money?
jokes.setup: Bart was spending money recklessly
jokes.punchline: They apparently kissed for money but never got paid
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation with good character work showing their casual attitude about the situation

jokes.text: But reckless spending and interracial homoeroticism were just volume one of The Encyclopedia Self-Destructica.
jokes.setup: Narrator listing the family's problems
jokes.punchline: Fake encyclopedia title that sounds academic but describes their dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Creative fake title that captures the documentary style while describing escalating problems

jokes.text: Then came the rocks - jagged rocks, hitting me with their jags.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his failed stunt jump
jokes.punchline: Redundant description of jagged rocks using made-up word 'jags'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism showing his limited vocabulary in describing pain

jokes.text: Let's just say that fame was like a drug. But what was even more like a drug was the drugs.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his painkiller addiction metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Abandons metaphor for literal truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with Homer's literal-mindedness undermining cliche metaphor

jokes.text: I mean, there we were with Hollywood royalty like Milton Berle and Nelson Mandela.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing the honor of being on Hollywood Walk of Fame
jokes.punchline: Lists comedian and political prisoner/president as equivalent Hollywood royalty
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor mixing entertainment figures with world leaders as if equivalent

jokes.text: And just a few stars down from the Cheerios honeybee.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of Walk of Fame neighbors
jokes.punchline: Corporate mascot has a star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurdist theme of random entities getting Hollywood recognition

jokes.text: Awards and honors are great, but they don't pay the pickle man.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing awards in favor of practical concerns
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'pay the bills' or 'piper' - 'pickle man'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer malapropism that creates absurd image while conveying his meaning

jokes.text: Her check bounced higher than Rubber Girl.
jokes.setup: Lisa's expensive purchase caused financial problems
jokes.punchline: Bounced check compared to fictional super-bouncy character
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor mixing financial terminology with imaginary bouncing character

jokes.text: When people reach for their diaphragm, they don't want to see my picture.
jokes.setup: Marge lost money investing in birth control
jokes.punchline: Her image on contraceptives was counterproductive to romance
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about the unsexy nature of birth control marketing with maternal figures

jokes.text: If the sea captain could be in the show, why not me? I've even got a catchphrase. Nyah! Whaa! Whaa, whaa, whaa! Now I lost it.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy explaining how he got on the show
jokes.punchline: His terrible catchphrase that he immediately forgets
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing desperate attempt at showbiz relevance with immediate failure

jokes.text: Yes, I finked on Homer, but, you know, he deserved it. Never have I seen such abuse of the 'Take a penny, leave a penny' tray.
jokes.setup: Apu admits to reporting the Simpsons for tax evasion
jokes.punchline: His justification is trivial penny tray abuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-driven humor with Apu's pettiness about small change vs. major tax evasion

jokes.text: Hey! They can't take our house! My potbellied pig is in there! Ah, Mr. Porky! No!
jokes.setup: IRS is seizing their house and possessions
jokes.punchline: Homer's main concern is his pet pig
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's misplaced priorities during crisis

jokes.text: Fine. I'll do Teen Wolf 3. I've got fair-weather friends to feed.
jokes.setup: Bart reluctantly agreeing to continue working
jokes.punchline: Admits his friends are only there for his money
jokes.explanation: Good cynical observation about child stardom and fake friendships

jokes.text: Hey, you've been riding me all day. Why don't you poop in your hat?
jokes.setup: Family argument during filming
jokes.punchline: Bart's bizarre insult suggestion
jokes.explanation: Absurd insult that sounds like something a stressed-out kid would say

jokes.text: That man is the real Seymour Skinner.
jokes.setup: Narrator discussing gimmicky plot premises
jokes.punchline: Reference to actual controversial Simpsons episode about Skinner's identity
jokes.explanation: Self-referential joke about the show's own controversial storytelling decisions

jokes.text: While he was in rehab, the part of Bart Simpson was played by his good friend Richie Rich.
jokes.setup: Bart had problems and couldn't work
jokes.punchline: Cartoon character Richie Rich filled in for him
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist solution using another cartoon character as replacement actor

jokes.text: Don't have a cow, Mother.
jokes.setup: Richie Rich playing Bart Simpson
jokes.punchline: Uses Bart's catchphrase but in his own refined speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between characters' speech patterns while using familiar catchphrase

jokes.text: Hello, Iowa! Does anyone have a doughnut?
jokes.setup: Family performing live at Iowa State Fair
jokes.punchline: Homer's opening is immediately self-serving and off-topic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment showing his priorities even during performance

jokes.text: Before a riot could break out, Jimmy Carter came to the rescue with his comedy break dancing.
jokes.setup: Audience getting angry at family fight
jokes.punchline: Former president's bizarre entertainment intervention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image combining unlikely person with unlikely skill for crowd control

jokes.text: Coming up: Was the dream really over? Yes, it was. Or was it?
jokes.setup: Typical documentary tease format
jokes.punchline: Immediately answers own rhetorical question then doubts it
jokes.explanation: Good parody of documentary teaser format with contradictory information

jokes.text: But now the wing was on fire, and the prayer had been answered - by Satan.
jokes.setup: Continuing wing and prayer metaphor from earlier
jokes.punchline: Dark twist that Satan answered their prayers
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier metaphor with dark comedic twist

jokes.text: Fox put the show on hiatus and replaced it with hidden camera footage from the dressing room at Ann Taylor.
jokes.setup: Network needed replacement programming
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate surveillance at boring clothing store
jokes.explanation: Funny absurd replacement show concept combining voyeurism with mundane retail environment

jokes.text: I literally chewed the scenery.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing her theatrical performance
jokes.punchline: Takes acting cliche literally, implies she actually ate the set
jokes.explanation: Excellent literalization of common acting expression with perfect economy

jokes.text: So the next time you see a sheriff, shoot him. A smile.
jokes.setup: Marge performing nightclub act about shooting sheriff
jokes.punchline: Dark setup resolved with innocent clarification
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Marge's innocence even when trying to be edgy

jokes.text: How could I even get all five necessary drops into her cereal?
jokes.setup: Homer denying giving Lisa anti-growth hormones
jokes.punchline: His defense reveals specific knowledge of how to do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-incrimination structure where his denial proves his guilt with specific details

jokes.text: I mean, emotionally it was terrible, but the turkey was so moist.
jokes.setup: Describing dysfunctional Thanksgiving dinner
jokes.punchline: Focuses on food quality despite family trauma
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing elderly priorities and ability to find silver linings

jokes.text: That night, fate wore a cummerbund of suspense.
jokes.setup: Building suspense for awards show resolution
jokes.punchline: Overwrought metaphor with oddly specific formal wear
jokes.explanation: Creative overwrought metaphor typical of documentary narrator's dramatic style

jokes.text: Well, I'm also trying to patch things up between Van Halen and Sammy Hagar.
jokes.setup: Willie Nelson admits the awards show was fake to reunite the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: He's also working on unrelated band reconciliation
jokes.explanation: Good additional absurd detail showing Willie's multiple reconciliation projects

jokes.text: And now the future looks brighter than ever for this northern Kentucky family.
jokes.setup: Documentary conclusion about the Simpsons' future
jokes.punchline: Incorrectly identifies them as from Kentucky instead of Springfield
jokes.explanation: Subtle joke about documentary getting basic facts wrong about their subjects

jokes.text: This will be the last season.
jokes.setup: Family celebrating going to Delaware
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact announcement of the show's end
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke playing with audience expectations about the show's longevity

jokes.text: I was so gay. But I couldn't tell anyone.
jokes.setup: Preview for next week's Behind the Laughter about Huckleberry Hound
jokes.punchline: Cartoon character's confession using old meaning of 'gay' (happy) vs. modern meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke exploiting the evolution of word meaning in different eras

tmdb_overview: A "Behind the Music"-style look at the Simpsons, narrated by VH1's Jim Forbes, shows their rise to stardom--and the "private hell" that followed.
